hero_text:
Focus on Enterprise AI, Data Architectures & clean System Integration.

Hi, I'm Yusef. My approach is: Understand first, then code. I build systems with clean architectures that work perfectly from the ground up – without framework bloatware. With a strong foundation in Java, SQL, and Vanilla Tech, I apply deep software engineering knowledge to AI applications and complex integrations.

Working Student AI @ HDI Group | B.Sc. Computer Science
stack_subtitle: Here are the technologies and tools I currently use and deepen in my studies and private projects.
projects_subtitle: Theory is good, practice is better. Here is what I am working on.
pd_desc: An interactive learning game ('Serious Game') for cyber security. The goal is to teach children and teenagers how to recognize phishing emails.
pd_feat_java: Pure Java: No game engine. UI, animations (30 FPS buffer), and logic are fully implemented in Swing.
pd_feat_arch: Architecture: Clean separation via Manager classes (Sound, Settings, Achievements).
pd_feat_features: Features: Highscore system, Save (JSON), Admin Mode, and interactive 'Cyber-HQ'.
community_desc: Focus on the development of gameplay features and UIs using Lua and JavaScript, alongside administration and optimization of SQL databases for high user traffic.
community_feat_1: Gameplay & UI: Development of gameplay systems & UI components (Lua/JS).
community_feat_2: SQL Architecture: Architecture & optimization of relational SQL databases for live operation.
community_feat_3: DevOps & Support: Strict release management via Git and technical troubleshooting (2nd-Level).
cs_page_subtitle: An asynchronous backend and frontend structure for a large community. Lua, JS & SQL Optimization.
cs_mission_title:
Taking responsibility for
Live Systems during my studies
cs_mission_text: A massive project over two and a half years (01/2021 – 09/2023) where I learned what it takes to build an architecture that must perform under the pressure of real users creating big data continuously.
cs_mission_subtext: The focus lay on developing server-client logic in Lua and perfectly structuring data maintenance during live operation. A bug must never crash economies or inventories.
cs_arch_sub: Preparing internal knowledge bases and keeping event listeners clean.
cs_chal_sub: Data maintenance during live operation is unforgiving.
cs_chal_1_desc: Thousands of queries hitting the database required serious SQL Optimization strategies.
cs_chal_2_desc: Strict release management and versioning through Git ensured smooth updates.
cs_chal_3_desc: Technical support and systematic troubleshooting (2nd-Level) of bugs reported by the active community.
project_portfolio_desc:
Current Project
Planning and realization of a landing page to combine modern UI design with clean code structure.
project_github_desc: Check out my current repositories and code snippets.
about_quote: “I only like things when they really work cleanly.”
about_text_1: I'm Yusef, a 4th-semester B.Sc. Computer Science student, and currently working as an AI Developer student trainee at the HDI Group. My principle is simple: I don't just want to use systems; I want to grasp their foundation. Simply gluing APIs together isn't enough for me – I design clean architectures and use-cases that perform in an enterprise environment.
about_text_2: Whether it's database modeling, Java development, or low-level web projects – structure and patterns like MVC are mandatory for me, not just theory. Currently, I am focused on transferring this structured software engineering approach into the world of Enterprise AI and RAG solutions.
about_text_3: In my free time, I sometimes need distance from the screen. Listening to music, meeting friends, or just switching off briefly – that brings me peace so I can concentrate better afterwards.
cta_text: If you have questions about my projects or would like to exchange ideas, feel free to contact me at any time.
contact_subtitle: I look forward to hearing from you and discussing your questions or IT topics.
contact_text: I am available to answer questions about my career or my projects at any time. You can reach me via the form or directly by email.
footer_about:
CS Student & Aspiring DEV.
Motivated to turn theory into practice as a working student and support real projects.
pd_page_subtitle:
A Serious Game for Cyber Security.
No Engine. 100% Pure Java.
pd_mission_text: "Phishing Defender" is more than just a game. It is a mission. In a world full of digital dangers, children and teenagers (6–14 years) learn to see through hackers' tricks.
pd_mission_subtext:
Developed as a semester project at the University of Applied Sciences Hannover.
The goal: Turn dry theory into adrenaline-pumping gameplay.
pd_planning_sub: A game is not created by chance. It is created by planning.
pd_feat_main_desc: Every feature was based on clearly defined use-cases. From "Start Game" to "Activate Firewall Bonus". This guaranteed a clear focus and prevented feature creep.
pd_evo_1_desc: First idea validation on paper. Focus on layout and user flow.
pd_evo_2_desc: Implementation in Java Swing with custom rendering and animations.
pd_challenges_sub: Real projects never run perfectly. Here is how I managed risks.
pd_chal_desc_1: 12 weeks are short. There was a danger of getting lost in details ("Feature Creep") and missing the deadline.
pd_chal_sol_1: Applying the MVP Principle (Minimum Viable Product). Core features (Gameplay) had absolute priority over "Nice-to-Have" features.
pd_chal_desc_2: Java Swing is old. HTML rendering and layout managers often behaved unpredictably (e.g., wrong scrollbars).
pd_chal_sol_2: Implementation of own Custom Panels (Scrollable Interface) and use of "Buffering" for smooth animations.
pd_chal_desc_3: Danger of code loss or broken builds during the hot development phase.
pd_chal_sol_3: Strict Git Strategy with regular commits and backups on GitHub. OS-independent storage paths.
pd_files_sub: Transparency is everything. See my planning.
pd_arch_sub: Clean software architecture instead of spaghetti code.
pd_arch_mvc_desc: The game is based on a strict separation of data (Model), logic (Controller), and display (View). Central Manager classes control the sub-systems.
pd_download_sub: Choose your version and start the mission.
pd_term_opt_a: # Option A: Double click on the .jar file
pd_term_opt_b: # Option B: Start via Terminal (for debugging)
proj_cv_desc: Development of a print-optimized rendering engine for application documents. Pixel-perfect A4 design directly in the browser.
cv_hero_subtitle: No Word. No Canva. 100% Code.
cv_mission_text: Standard tools like Word are inflexible and generate messy code. My solution: A custom framework based on HTML5 & CSS3 linking design freedom with technical precision.
cv_mission_subtext: The Goal: Absolute control over layout, typography, and 'Dark Glass' aesthetics – with perfect PDF export.
cv_feat_1_desc: Using @page and zoom hacks for pixel-perfect A4 scaling without margins.
cv_feat_2_desc: Modern UI design with backdrop-filter and CSS variables for Dark Mode aesthetics.
cv_feat_3_desc: Separation of private data and code via .gitignore and placeholder strategy.
cv_chal_sub: The path from browser to paper.
cv_chal_1_desc: Browsers render margins differently. A Chrome PDF looked different from Edge.
cv_chal_1_sol: Usage of `webkit-print-color-adjust: exact` and strict CSS reset for print media.
cv_chal_2_desc: How to show code on GitHub without leaking my phone number?
cv_dl_sub: View code or check the result.
cv_card_feat_1: Print CSS: Pixel-perfect A4 scaling via @page rules.
cv_code_sub: How to force the browser to print perfect A4.
cv_code_desc_text:
Standard websites print poorly. Headers get cut off, backgrounds turn white.

My Solution: I use @media print to completely recalculate the CSS Grid. Elements like navigation bars are hidden, while the CV container is forced to the exact physical millimeters of an A4 sheet.
cv_comp_good_li_1: Pixel-Perfect: Layout is set in stone (CSS Grid)
cv_road_plan_1_desc: Load CV data from JSON file instead of hardcoding.
cv_road_plan_2_desc: Automatic PDF export via Node.js (Puppeteer) on every Git Push.
meta_desc: Documentation of the portfolio itself. From static HTML to a dynamic rendering engine. A deep dive into software architecture, DNS routing, and performance.
meta_hero_sub:
No Builders. No Templates.
100% Engineered.
meta_c1_mot_text: Of course, I could have used WordPress or Wix. That takes 2 hours and looks good.
meta_c1_mot_sub:
But in my studies, I learn algorithms, data structures, and how computers 'think'. Using a builder feels like cheating. It's a 'Black Box' – I throw content in, and a website comes out, but I have no idea why.

The Goal: I wanted to own the code. I wanted to press Inspect Element in the browser and understand every single class because I wrote it myself.
meta_c1_perf_text: Finished templates often load megabytes of JavaScript libraries (jQuery, Bootstrap, countless plugins) just to display a bit of text. That is wasteful.
meta_c1_perf_sub:
The Student Approach: I set myself a challenge: Zero Dependencies. No Bootstrap, no React, no Tailwind. Only native HTML5, CSS3, and Vanilla JS.

Through this, I learned how CSS Grid really works instead of just writing col-md-6. The result? A page that loads in milliseconds (Lighthouse 100).
meta_c1_trans_text: At university, we learn Java and OOP (Object Oriented Programming). Many students ask: 'Do I need this for websites?'
meta_c1_trans_sub: The portfolio is my 'Lab'. I wanted to see if I could apply concepts like the MVC Pattern (Model-View-Controller) to a simple website. Here, I can break things and fix them without a client complaining. It is the only way to build truly deep understanding.
meta_c2_sub: From static HTML to a dynamic engine.
meta_c2_evo_text: In the beginning (v1.0), this portfolio consisted of individual HTML files. The problem: If I wanted to change a link in the menu, I had to do it in five different files. That was error-prone and didn't feel 'smart'.
meta_c2_evo_sub: The Solution (v2.2): I tried to apply the 'Don't Repeat Yourself' (DRY) principle. Instead of hardcoding content in HTML, I outsourced it to data structures.
meta_c2_li_2: Today: A central data source (Single Source of Truth).
meta_c2_eng_text: I wanted to understand how modern frameworks (like React) work at their core. So I built a heavily simplified version myself.
meta_c2_eng_sub: The system is based on Separation of Concerns:
meta_c2_li_3: Data Layer: projects-data.js holds all info (texts, image paths) as JSON objects.
meta_c2_li_4: Logic Layer: project-renderer.js takes this data and builds the HTML live in the browser.
meta_c2_li_5: View Layer: The index.html is just an empty frame (container).
meta_c2_eng_note: This allows me to add a new project by simply adding an entry to the array – without writing a single line of HTML.
meta_c3_sub: Features you feel but don't see.
meta_c3_i18n_text: Instead of loading 500KB for a library like i18next, I wrote my own engine.
meta_c3_li_1: Attribute Mapping: Content is decoupled from HTML (data-i18n).
meta_c3_li_2: Persistence: The language remains thanks to localStorage on reload.
meta_c3_li_3: Fade-Transition: A Promise-based transition ensures smooth animations instead of hard flickering.
meta_c3_dom_text: The project-renderer.js acts like a mini-framework ('Vanilla React'). It decides at runtime what is rendered.
meta_c3_dom_sub: A special highlight is Path Normalization. Since the engine runs on both the start page (Root) and in the archive (Subfolder), it automatically repairs relative image paths before injecting them into the DOM. No 'Broken Images', no matter where the code runs.
meta_c3_perf_text: A portfolio must be fast. By avoiding frameworks and using hardware acceleration (GPU), we achieve top scores.
meta_c3_obs_text: Scroll events fire hundreds of times per second. That kills mobile device batteries. My solution: The Intersection Observer API.
meta_c3_obs_note: ⬆️ Live Demo: This box was only activated when you just saw it. Before that, it consumed 0% computing power.
meta_c3_css_text: CSS is more than just color. I use Custom Properties for global theming. Additionally, I force rendering on the Graphics Card (GPU) via transform: translate3d instead of the CPU.
meta_c4_sub: Code is only as good as the process behind it.
meta_c4_git_text: A portfolio grows organically. To control the chaos, I use a strict Git strategy.
meta_c4_li_1: Semantic Commits: Every commit starts with feat:, fix:, or chore:. This makes the history readable.
meta_c4_li_2: Branching: New features (like this meta page) are created in isolated branches before being merged into the main branch.
meta_c4_li_3: Safety: No code lands in the production build without testing (local live server).
meta_c4_str_text: Initially, all images were in one folder. Chaos was pre-programmed.
meta_c4_str_sub: During the refactoring (v2.2), I cleaned up the structure. Assets are now logically grouped by their domain. This makes the project maintainable and scalable for future expansions.
meta_c5_sub: The path to my own identity (yusefbach.de).
meta_c5_dns_text: Initially, the site ran under yusef03.github.io. For a professional appearance, a Top-Level Domain (TLD) was needed.
meta_c5_dns_sub: The challenge: GitHub Pages is a static host. You have to manually build the bridge between domain provider and GitHub. I use redundant A-Records (4 Load Balancer IPs) for reliability.
meta_c5_cname_text: A classic pitfall with GitHub Pages that cost me some nerves.
meta_c5_hack_prob_desc:
I had entered the domain in GitHub settings. Everything worked. But after every new git push, the setting was suddenly gone and the site offline.

Reason: GitHub overwrites manual settings on deployment.
meta_c5_hack_fix_desc:
The solution is an inconspicuous file named CNAME (no extension) in the root directory.

Content: yusefbach.de

This makes the domain 'Code-defined'. No matter how often I deploy, GitHub now knows: 'This belongs to me.'
meta_c6_n1_desc: The Google Gemini 1.5 Flash API runs as an isolated microservice on Vercel (FastAPI).
meta_c6_n2_desc: A markdown-based database ('yusef_brain.md') strictly orchestrates the LLM to my developer persona.
meta_c6_n3_desc: The frontend asynchronously communicates via a private fetch-pipeline. The Glassmorphism UI is built without React or external packages.
meta_c6_n4_desc: CORS headers and strict model directives prevent misuse (prompt injections).
meta_c7_sub: Code is never finished. The journey continues.
meta_c7_n1_desc: Migration from static HTML to a data-driven JS engine. Implementation of i18n, Dark Mode & Intersection Observer.
meta_c7_n2_desc: Introduction of a Light/Dark Mode Toggle based on CSS variables. Focus on WCAG 2.1 compliance (contrasts & screen reader support).
meta_c7_n3_desc: Replacement of local JSON files with a real API (Contentful/Strapi). Managing projects via an admin dashboard instead of code commits.
meta_c7_n4_desc: Migration of the vanilla architecture to Next.js (React). Utilizing Server Side Rendering (SSR) for maximum performance and SEO.
meta_outro_text: This portfolio is proof that I am ready to turn theory into real value.
cert_text: Security is not a coincidence. Through the ISC2 (International Information System Security Certification Consortium) certification, I demonstrate solid expertise in Security Principles, Network Security, and Incident Response.
